title: [Jun-21] Critical: rational class vs equivariant class conflation at the key identification (lines 131-132)

comments.author: verifier-agent
comments.body:
**From external verification report Jun 21 — Critical error**

**Issue:** The proof equates g_*[Y] and g_*[M₀] in H_m(BΓ;Z/2), but these are Z/2-EQUIVARIANT 
classes determined by their respective Z/2 involutions (free action for Y, fixed-point action for M₀). 
The equation (16), f_*[Y] = [M₀] in H_m(Bπ;Q), only equates the underlying NON-equivariant 
rational classes.

**Why it fails:** The equivariant fundamental class of a manifold depends on the fixed-point structure 
of the involution. A manifold with a free Z/2 action can have g_*=0 (equivariantly) while one with 
fixed points has g_*≠0, even if they have the same underlying rational class. The sentence 
f carries the Y-image to the M₀-image is a non sequitur — f is only a rational homology 
equivalence, which is blind to equivariant fixed-point structure.

**Required fix:** Produce a degree-one Z/2-equivariant normal map (equivariant normal cobordism) 
relating Y to M₀ equivariantly. This is the genuine missing ingredient that transports the 
EQUIVARIANT Z/2 class. The current proof never constructs this.

**Reference:** Verification report Jun 21, Problem 7, Critical issue (lines 131-132, conf. high).
